full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic quadcopter frame per the topology-optimization study 'Generate Quadcopter' open-CAD challenge (2017). Frame targets: total frame mass <= 90 g; support 4 BLDC motors symmetrically arranged on a 250 mm motor-to-motor diagonal; hold a 1000 mAh 3S Li-Po battery at a central location (battery mass 80 g, dimensions 73 x 34 x 16 mm); four ESCs mounted adjacent to motors; full-vehicle mass (frame + electronics + battery + propellers) 450 g; thrust-to-weight ratio T/W >= 2:1 so total thrust >= 9 N (approx 2.25 N per motor). Material: carbon-fiber-reinforced PA12 (nylon 12 with 40% short carbon fiber), 3D printed (MJF or SLS), E = 5 GPa, yield = 110 MPa, ultimate = 130 MPa, density = 1200 kg/m^3. Geometric constraints: motor mount pads 16 mm circle with 19 mm bolt-hole pattern (4x M3); central battery pocket 75 x 36 x 17 mm; overall frame bounding 280 mm x 280 mm x 25 mm. Load cases: (R1) Normal flight 1 g hover (forces balance, low stress): not binding but verified. (R2) 2 g pull-up: full 450 g x 2 g x 9.81 = 8.8 N distributed to four motor mounts = 2.2 N per mount upward; max von Mises in frame arm root <= yield / 2.0 = 55 MPa. (R3) Hard landing 10 g: 450 g x 10 g x 9.81 = 44 N at battery mount pushing down; max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 73 MPa. (R4) Crash impact: 50 N at one motor boom tip horizontally (prop strike); max von Mises <= ultimate / 1.5 = 87 MPa. (R5) Frame mass <= 90 g. (R6) First modal frequency cantilevered at battery mount / central hub >= 80 Hz (to avoid coupling with motor 1P frequency at approximately 40 Hz at 2400 RPM). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x3 cases) + modal. Vibration secondary via R6. Thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid, build orientation, and a report listing frame mass in g, max von Mises per case in MPa, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic quadcopter frame per the topology-optimization study 'Generate Quadcopter' open-CAD challenge (2017). Frame is carbon-fiber-reinforced PA12 (40% short CF nylon 12), 3D printed (MJF or SLS). Supports 4 BLDC motors symmetrically on a 250 mm motor-to-motor diagonal; holds a 1000 mAh 3S Li-Po battery (80 g, 73 x 34 x 16 mm) in a central pocket; four ESCs sit next to the motors. Full-vehicle mass (frame + electronics + battery + propellers) 450 g. Thrust-to-weight >= 2:1 so total thrust >= 9 N (~2.25 N per motor).

Envelope: bounding 280 x 280 x 25 mm. Motor mount pads 16 mm circles with 19 mm 4 x M3 bolt pattern; battery pocket 75 x 36 x 17 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 1 g hover (balance check, not binding).
- LC2 2 g pull-up: 8.8 N distributed across the 4 motor mounts (2.2 N per mount upward).
- LC3 hard landing 10 g: 44 N at the battery mount pushing down.
- LC4 crash impact: 50 N horizontal at one motor-boom tip (prop strike, ultimate case).
- LC5 modal: cantilevered at the central hub / battery mount, first frequency >= 80 Hz (clear of ~40 Hz motor 1P at 2400 RPM).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 90 g mass cap is aggressive; Frustum challenge explicitly scored on generative design.

prompt.title: Frustum Generative Quadcopter frame
prompt.brief: CF-PA12 monolithic frame <= 90 g, 250 mm diagonal, 450 g vehicle, T/W >= 2:1.

boundary_conditions.mounting: central battery mount constrained for modal; motor-mount pads fixed for structural

exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Frame is bending-dominated with short arms.

eval_coverage: generative design on a 90 g mass cap
